sections: [FIFA U-20 World Cup](/wiki/FIFA_U-20_World_Cup "FIFA U-20 World Cup") Performances of Qualified South American teams
first_paragraph: The **South American Youth Football Championship**,[[a]](#cite_note-1) branded as **CONMEBOL Sub 20**, is a South American [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") tournament organized by the [CONMEBOL](/wiki/CONMEBOL "CONMEBOL") for South American national teams of men under age of 20. This tournament also serves as qualification for the [FIFA U-20 World Cup](/wiki/FIFA_U-20_World_Cup "FIFA U-20 World Cup") and the [Pan American Games](/wiki/Football_at_the_Pan_American_Games "Football at the Pan American Games"), and from 2007 to 2015, also for the [Summer Olympic Games](/wiki/Football_at_the_Summer_Olympics "Football at the Summer Olympics").

The **South American Youth Football Championship**,[[a]](#cite_note-1) branded as **CONMEBOL Sub 20**, is a South American [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") tournament organized by the [CONMEBOL](/wiki/CONMEBOL "CONMEBOL") for South American national teams of men under age of 20. This tournament also serves as qualification for the [FIFA U-20 World Cup](/wiki/FIFA_U-20_World_Cup "FIFA U-20 World Cup") and the [Pan American Games](/wiki/Football_at_the_Pan_American_Games "Football at the Pan American Games"), and from 2007 to 2015, also for the [Summer Olympic Games](/wiki/Football_at_the_Summer_Olympics "Football at the Summer Olympics").

The first South American Youth Championship was hosted by [Venezuela](/wiki/Venezuela "Venezuela") from 22 March to 13 April 1954. Initially played as an under-19 tournament, it became an under-20 event from 1977. Brazil has won the tournament on the most occasions (13 times).[[1]](#cite_note-rsf-2)

All matches take place in the host country, and all ten U-20 national football teams of [CONMEBOL](/wiki/CONMEBOL "CONMEBOL") compete in every edition (if none of the associations withdraw). They are separated in two groups of five, and each team plays four matches in a pure [round-robin](/wiki/Round-robin_tournament "Round-robin tournament") stage. The three top competitors advance to a single final group of six, wherein each team plays five matches. The results in this last pure round-robin stage determines the champion and the South American qualification to the next [FIFA U-20 World Cup](/wiki/FIFA_U-20_World_Cup "FIFA U-20 World Cup"). Unlike most international tournaments, in South American Youth Championships there is neither final match nor third place match nor [knockout](/wiki/Single-elimination_tournament "Single-elimination tournament") stages.
